rationale: The evidence supports function contrast, not one unified construction or exhaustive inventory.
The lexical GIVE baseline is externally supported, but broad distribution and alternative order remain review-bearing.
Predicate class, semantic role, weight, discourse, and speaker variation prohibit a global order switch.
The surface pattern is attested, but transfer, Goal, beneficiary, serial, and category analyses must remain distinct or unresolved.
The evidence records a tendency with conflicting judgments, sparse corpus cells, and no licensed threshold.
Attested omission and restructuring justify caution, not participant invention or a productive parser rule.
CP020R2 already owns the provisional passive-permissive relation; CP021B evidence may later reconstruct provenance but cannot backdate origin or bypass rendered review.
This project representation disposition forbids recreating the unsupported legacy unification under a new label.
The available corpora, tasks, and samples do not support a current unrestricted-adult conversational frequency model.
